Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

commit.message:
fix(ci): fix Python virtualenv and install plugin validators

maturin develop requires an active virtualenv — add a Create virtualenv
step that sets VIRTUAL_ENV and adds bin/Scripts to PATH for all
subsequent steps. Works cross-platform via GITHUB_ENV/GITHUB_PATH.

Plugin validation was failing because PLUGINVAL_PATH and
CLAP_VALIDATOR_PATH were set to paths that didn't exist (tools never
downloaded). Add install steps for pluginval v1.0.4 and clap-validator
0.3.2 before running cargo xtask verify-plugins. Move the env vars to
the validate step so they only apply when the tools are present.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove Date.now() from chunk filenames — use content hash only

Date.now() renamed every chunk on every deploy, immediately
breaking open tabs regardless of whether content changed.
Content-based [hash] means unchanged files keep the same
filename across deploys, so the existing unhandledrejection
chunk-reload handler only fires when content actually changed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: eliminate phantom position alerts from double-counted reconciliation (#1217)

* fix: eliminate phantom position alerts from double-counted reconciliation entries

When phantom reconciliation creates synthetic close entries BEFORE Flex
reconciliation discovers the real trades, both entries account for the
same position close, causing get_local_active_positions() to report
non-zero net positions and triggering persistent false alerts.

Add invalidate_superseded_synthetics() that detects (local_symbol, action)
pairs where both synthetic and RECONCILIATION_MISSING entries exist, and
writes idempotent reversal entries to cancel the superseded synthetics.
Called automatically after trade ledger reconciliation in main().

Verified against production KC data: all 3 phantom positions (KON6 C2.8,
C2.85, P2.8) eliminated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove unnecessary wiring of invalidation into main()

main() won't create RECONCILIATION_MISSING entries for trades already
covered by synthetics (they match during comparison). The double-counting
only exists in historical data already fixed by the one-time run.
Keep invalidate_superseded_synthetics() as a standalone utility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: re-wire invalidation into main() — synthetics never match real trades

Synthetic entries have fabricated timestamps (phantom reconciliation time,
not actual trade time), so reconcile_trades() 2-second tolerance never
matches them against real IB trades. main() DOES write RECONCILIATION_MISSING
entries for trades already covered by synthetics, causing double-counting.

The invalidation step after write_missing_trades_to_csv() is needed to
detect and cancel superseded synthetics on every reconciliation cycle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: containerMenu b/r chaining, NULL comparisons, Math.floor truncation

Three categories of C-to-JS translation fixes:

1. pickup.js: Extract doTakeOut/doPutIn helpers from containerMenu so 'b'
   (both) and 'r' (reverse) can call them sequentially, matching C's
   use_container() flow. Removes pendingAction/chainedAction mechanism
   that failed because 'o' handler returned before chained action ran.
   Also adds throwOnEmpty option to createInputQueue() in input.js.

2. NULL comparison fixes (8 sites, 8 files): C's NULL==0 means `ptr != 0`
   tests non-NULL, but JS `null !== 0` is always true. Fixed wizard.js,
   cmd.js, dog.js, monmove.js, pager.js, do_wear.js, glyphs.js,
   detect.js, makemon.js to use `!= null` or `= null`.

3. Math.floor integer division (18 sites, 14 files): C int/int truncates
   automatically; JS division produces floats. Added Math.floor() in
   uhitm.js (critical parenthesis bug), makemon.js, shk.js, exper.js,
   spell.js, potion.js, artifact.js, polyself.js, mon.js, trap.js,
   botl.js, rip.js, calendar.js, sp_lev.js. Simplified constant
   expressions in dog.js (40/2→20 etc.).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: persist work note fixture links to Supabase so all users see hookup info

Links between work notes and fixtures were only stored in localStorage,
making hookup info visible only to the user who created the links.
Now reads/writes to the work_note_fixture_links table, with automatic
migration of existing localStorage links on first load.

commit.message:
Fix reminder email_log type to match DB constraint

The email_log table has a check constraint allowing 'reminder' but
the scheduler was logging 'reminder_1' and manual send was logging
'reminder_manual', which silently failed the constraint. Changed both
to use 'reminder' so log entries are actually persisted and timestamps
show up in the Email Controls panel.

commit.message:
feat: Ordered gap buffer + Fenwick tree, benchmarks, fix iterator deadlock (#29)

* feat: rewrite Ordered with gap buffer + Fenwick tree, add benchmarks

Ordered.Remove improves from O(N) to O(log N) amortized using a gap
buffer with a Fenwick tree (binary indexed tree) for rank queries.
At(i) and Index(m) change from O(1) to O(log N). Auto-compacts when
gaps exceed 2x alive elements.

Add comprehensive benchmarks:
- bench_test.go: standard Go benchmarks for all 5 implementations
- bench_stats_test.go: statistical benchmarks (BENCH_STATS=1) outputting
  CSV with min/max/avg/stddev/percentiles
- bench_plot.py: generates per-operation PNG charts from CSV
- bench_stats.csv: baseline results (660 records)
- bench_charts/: 11 PNG charts

Also fix zero-value style: use `var x T` instead of `x := 0`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use snapshot iteration in Locked/LockedOrdered to prevent deadlock

Iterator, Ordered, and Backwards methods on Locked and LockedOrdered
previously held a read lock for the entire duration of iteration. This
caused a deadlock when the yield callback called any mutating method
(Add, Remove, Clear, Pop) on the same set, since sync.RWMutex is not
reentrant.

Now these methods snapshot the elements under a read lock, release the
lock, then iterate the snapshot. This eliminates the deadlock while
preserving point-in-time consistency. The tradeoff is an O(n) allocation
per iteration call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: move benchmark artifacts to benchmarks/ dir, add charts to README

Move bench_plot.py, bench_stats.csv, and bench_charts/ into a
benchmarks/ subdirectory. Add a Benchmarks section to the README
with instructions for running benchmarks and all 11 charts showing
performance across implementations and set sizes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address Copilot review feedback

- Add TestOrdered_AtIndex_AfterRemoval: targeted test exercising At()
  and Index() after removals, verifying Fenwick tree consistency both
  before and after compaction.
- Fix writeStatsCSV: check csv.Writer.Error() after Flush and propagate
  f.Close() errors instead of silently swallowing write failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Attention patches: Hebbian-controlled attention output modification

Add attention output patches alongside existing residual patches. Each patched
layer now has two hooks: one on self_attn (modifies information routing) and one
on the decoder layer (existing residual patch). Both use separate
LearnedPlasticityRule instances with eligibility traces + RPE-gated plasticity.

- AttentionPatch: DifferentiablePatch with d_hidden=64 (subtle corrections)
- Separate attn_hebb_rule with its own traces, compress, PlasticityNet
- Total params: 2.3M → 3.3M (+1M for attention plasticity)
- Updated truncation boundary detachment for attention state
- Updated save/restore for validation checkpoints

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(hitl): HookResponse protocol + dynamic permission buttons (#69)

D1 — HookResponse protocol:
- Shared/IPCProtocol: HookResponse: Codable com decision/ptyKey e statics .allowOnce/.allowSession/.deny/.ask
- IPCClient: lê length-prefixed JSON (4 bytes + body) em vez de 1 byte
- HookHandler: switch em response.decision para stdout JSON e exit code
- HookIPCServer: respondHITL(response:) serializa HookResponse com length-prefix
- SessionManager: approveHITL(response:) + rejectHITL + showInTerminalHITL; PTY input via ptyKey no response

D2 — Dynamic buttons + Show in terminal:
- Shared/IPCProtocol: permissionSuggestions: [String]? em HookPayload e AgentEvent
- HookHandler: extrai permission_suggestions e repassa no AgentEvent
- SessionManager: pendingSuggestions: [String] em AgentSession
- ApprovalCardView: PermissionSuggestion struct; HITLItem com suggestions/onShowInTerminal; actionRow dinâmico com fallback Approve/Reject
- HITLFloatingPanelController: buildSuggestions(for:) mapeia yes-session → allowSession, reject → deny, default → allowOnce

commit.message:
feat: Veblen org migration scripts + fix hardcoded orgId in withOrgScope

Problem: existing users had no org membership, and withOrgScope fell back
to a hardcoded orgId ('org_1757046595553') which won't survive a real migration.

Changes:
- backend/scripts/migrate-to-veblen.js
    Idempotent migration that:
    1. Finds or creates the Veblen organization
    2. Creates memberships for ALL users who don't have one
       (admin@eversense.ai → OWNER, everyone else → STAFF)
    3. Backfills every data table where orgId points to a
       non-existent organization (orphaned records → Veblen)

- backend/scripts/verify-veblen.js
    Post-migration checker that confirms:
    - Every user has a Veblen membership
    - Every data table has zero orphaned orgIds
    Prints ✅/❌ per table with IDs of any remaining bad rows

- backend/lib/rbac.js — withOrgScope
    Replace hardcoded 'org_1757046595553' emergency fallback with a real
    DB lookup (prisma.membership.findFirst for the authenticated user).
    After migration every user has a Veblen membership so this always
    resolves correctly without any hardcoded values.
    Also made the function async to support the DB lookup.

Run order:
  cd backend && node scripts/migrate-to-veblen.js
  cd backend && node scripts/verify-veblen.js

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
